title: Genosiris limbata

This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve skin health, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Genosiris limbata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Genosiris limbata is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in topical applications to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Genosiris limbata is classified as a species within the genus Genosiris, which is a small genus of flowering plants. The genus Genosiris is part of the family Iridaceae, commonly known as the iris family. Genus Genosiris is a monotypic genus, meaning it contains only one species, Genosiris limbata. This species is native to New Caledonia, a region in the Pacific Ocean. Genosiris limbata is considered an endemic species, meaning it is found only in its native region and nowhere else.
